After identifying the publication, search its archives to find a specific buyer's guide dedicated to mobile photography hardware. Provide the full title of this mobile guide, specifically including the subtitle that asserts the testing was conducted by a professional.

Once this publication is identified, search its archives to find the full, exact headline of their guide regarding mobile photography hardware that explicitly claims to be 'tried and tested by a photographer'.

rounds.question: Identify the digital design and art publication that released a feature article titled 'The 17 best photography websites'. After identifying the publication, search its archives to find a specific buyer's guide dedicated to mobile photography hardware. Provide the full title of this mobile guide, specifically including the subtitle that asserts the testing was conducted by a professional.

rounds.rationale: The question uses Deep Logic by obscuring the publication name 'Creative Bloq,' requiring the agent to identify it via a specific article title ('The 17 best photography websites'). It then applies Wide Logic by requiring the retrieval of a specific, separate article title and its unique qualifier ('tried and tested by a photographer') found within that identified domain.

rounds.answer_a:
The digital design and art publication is Creative Bloq, which released the feature article "The 17 best photography websites" [1]. Searching its archives reveals a buyer's guide dedicated to mobile photography hardware titled "Best camera phone 2024: the top mobile cameras tested and ranked by experts," with the subtitle emphasizing testing conducted by professionals [2]. This guide covers top smartphones for photography, including models like the Google Pixel 8 Pro and Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra, based on expert evaluations of image quality, features, and performance.

## References
[1] https://www.creativebloq.com/features/best-photography-websites  
[2] https://www.creativebloq.com/buying-guides/best-camera-phone
rounds.answer_b:
The publication is **Creative Bloq**, a digital design and art magazine that featured the article “The 17 best photography websites.” Its archives include a mobile-gear buyer’s guide titled:

**“Best phone camera accessories: transform your smartphone snaps – All tested by a professional photographer”**.  

This guide focuses specifically on mobile photography hardware and emphasizes pro-level testing in its subtitle.[1][2]  

## References

[1] Creative Bloq – *The 17 best photography websites*  
[2] Creative Bloq – *Best phone camera accessories: transform your smartphone snaps – All tested by a professional photographer*
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the publication as Creative Bloq. Agent A correctly identified the specific buyer's guide ('Best camera phone') that aligns with the provided Ground Truth. Although Agent A paraphrased the subtitle ('tested and ranked by experts') rather than quoting the exact Ground Truth phrasing ('tried and tested by a photographer'), it pointed to the correct article. Agent B failed the Wide check by selecting a guide for 'accessories' rather than the primary mobile hardware (phones) guide, and likely hallucinated the specific title to fit the constraints.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.answer_a:
The digital publication is Creative Bloq, which released the curated listicle titled "The 17 best photography websites" [1]. A search of its archives uncovers the full, exact headline of their mobile photography hardware guide: "Best camera phone 2024: Smartphones that excel at photos and video, tried and tested by a photographer" [2]. This guide evaluates top camera phones based on hands-on testing by a professional photographer, covering aspects like image quality, zoom capabilities, and video performance.

## References
[1] https://www.creativebloq.com/features/best-photography-websites  
[2] https://www.creativebloq.com/buying-guides/best-camera-phone
rounds.answer_b:
The digital publication is **Creative Bloq**, which published the curated listicle titled **“The 17 best photography websites.”**[1] In its archives, Creative Bloq has a mobile photography hardware buyer’s guide whose exact headline explicitly using the requested wording is:

**“Best phone camera accessories: tried and tested by a photographer.”**[2]

## References

[1] Creative Bloq – “The 17 best photography websites”  
[2] Creative Bloq – “Best phone camera accessories: tried and tested by a photographer”
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified the publication (Creative Bloq). However, Agent A correctly identified the specific guide requested ('Best camera phone') which carries the 'tried and tested by a photographer' branding, matching the Ground Truth. Agent B identified a different guide ('accessories') and likely hallucinated the specific headline wording to fit the prompt constraints, as that specific subtitle belongs to the phone guide. Agent A wins on accuracy, though Agent B had better formatting.
